reason:
The proof invokes 'by characterisation of the face generated by phi' to assert that psi in F_{S(A)}(phi) implies psi <= lambda*phi for some lambda > 0. This is a non-trivial implication that is neither proved inline nor listed as a dependency.

The argument requires: if psi in F_{S(A)}(phi), then by definition of the generated face, there exist sigma in S(A) and t in (0,1) such that phi = t*psi + (1-t)*sigma. Since sigma is a state (hence positive), for any a in A we get t*psi(a*a) <= phi(a*a), so psi(a*a) <= (1/t)*phi(a*a). This extends to psi <= (1/t)*phi as positive functionals, giving lambda = 1/t.

This derivation is straightforward but non-trivial and must be either: (a) proved inline, (b) extracted as a separate lemma node that 1.1.5 depends on, or (c) referenced as an external. Currently the node has NO dependencies, yet it relies on this fact and on the Radon-Nikodym theorem (definition Radon_Nikodym_for_states).

Note that node 1.1.3 uses the REVERSE direction of the same characterization (domination implies face membership) and has already been challenged (ch-ab3c20fa) for the same type of gap. Both directions of the equivalence 'psi in F_{S(A)}(phi) iff psi <= lambda*phi' should be established as a single lemma that both 1.1.3 and 1.1.5 depend on.

SUGGESTED FIX: (1) Create a new lemma node establishing the equivalence 'psi in F_{S(A)}(phi) iff there exists lambda > 0 with psi <= lambda*phi' with both directions proved. (2) Add this lemma as a dependency of both 1.1.3 and 1.1.5. (3) The forward direction proof: if phi = t*psi + (1-t)*sigma for 0 < t < 1, then psi <= (1/t)*phi since (1/t)*phi - psi = ((1-t)/t)*sigma >= 0. The reverse direction proof: if psi <= lambda*phi with lambda >= 1, then sigma := (lambda*phi - psi)/(lambda - 1) is a state (when lambda > 1), and phi = (1/lambda)*psi + (1 - 1/lambda)*sigma places psi in the face; when lambda = 1, psi <= phi and psi(1) = phi(1) = 1 forces psi = phi in face trivially.
